subject: Statistics
title: Sample Size and Precision
description: A quick, practical guide to Sample Size and Precision.

hook.text: A poll of 100 people has ±10% margin. A poll of 1,000 has ±3%. A poll of 10,000 has ±1%.

buildup.text: Precision improves with the square root of sample size. To halve the margin, you quadruple the sample.

discovery.text: This is why most polls use about 1,000 people. Beyond that, precision gains are tiny but costs soar.

twist.text: A perfect sample of 1,000 beats a biased sample of a million. Quality trumps quantity.

climax.text: The sweet spot between cost and precision is why you see '±3%' so often. It's the industry standard.

punchline.text: Bigger isn't always better. Better is better.

quiz.question: Why do most polls use about 1,000 respondents?
options: It gives ±3% margin — beyond that, precision gains are tiny but costs soar
It's the legal minimum for polls
Larger samples are always less accurate
